pending.body: In `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs`, the `reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings` function (around line 975) does NOT validate `mapped_to_bead_id` against the allowed PE set from the prompt, unlike both `engine.rs` (line 7641) and `final_review.rs` (line 1516-1519) which explicitly do. The reconstructed mappings go through `verify_bead_exists()` (which checks the bead exists in `br`) but this is not equivalent — a bead could exist but not be in the prompt's allowed PE set. While the final-review aggregate that the reconstruction reads from should already have been validated by `final_review.rs`, adding the same `extract_pe_bead_ids` check here would maintain the fail-closed invariant and protect against corrupted or tampered aggregate files on disk.

Validate `mapped_to_bead_id` before any planner/reviewer/arbiter voting prompts are built. In `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:644-651` and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:879-886`, the panel is asked to vote on amendments before the allowlist check runs. `build_voter_prompt` and `build_arbiter_prompt` then treat any non-empty mapping as planned-elsewhere at `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1894-1911` and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1951-1968`, including the promise that accepting it will not restart the bead. The actual validation only happens later at `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1516-1522`. A hallucinated or out-of-scope bead ID can therefore bias the panel into accepting an amendment under 'no restart' semantics, then get stripped and turned into a restart-required amendment afterward.

Fix planned-elsewhere reconstruction so later rounds can reconstruct the same finding/target pair. In `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:942-951` and `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:987-995`, `reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings` deduplicates only on `(active_bead_id, finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id)`. But `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:744-763` then throws away journal mappings from older completion rounds once a newer authoritative round exists. If round N recorded mapping X, round N+1 accepted the same X again, and only the round N+1 journal write failed, reconstruction skips it as 'already seen' and the latest-round mapping disappears entirely.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 20 -> Plan and Implement
